title: Cross-Domain Workflow (Simplified Input)
description: A user-friendly form to describe a multi-step workflow that may span manufacturing, characterization, simulation, and data management steps. Steps from different domain ontologies can be combined freely. Each step records its name, optional reference to a detailed knowledge-graph instance, and optional inline parameters. This input will be automatically converted to a standardised RDF representation (BFO/PMDCo OO-LD).

workflow_name.title: Workflow Name
workflow_name.description: A human-readable name for this workflow. Example: 'QA-to-FEM material card workflow — 316L batch 1'.

workflow_id.title: Workflow ID
workflow_id.description: Optional. IRI slug for the workflow node. Auto-derived from workflow_name if omitted.

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional free-text description of the overall workflow.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Ordered list of process steps. Steps are connected in sequence by default; use preceded_by to express branching.

step_id.title: Step ID
step_id.description: Optional. IRI slug for this step node. Auto-derived from label if omitted.

step_type.title: Step Type
step_type.description: Ontology class for this step (e.g. 'pmdco:PMD_0000029', 'obi:0000070', 'obi:0000471'). Leave blank for a generic BFO process step.

description.title: Step Description
description.description: Optional free-text description of what this step does.

instance_iri.title: Instance IRI
instance_iri.description: IRI of a fully described step instance in the knowledge graph. Include for provenance; omit for lightweight sketches.

preceded_by.title: Preceded By (Step IDs)
preceded_by.description: IDs of the steps that directly precede this one. If omitted, the previous step in the array is used.

conditions.title: Inline Parameters
conditions.description: Inline quantitative parameters for this step (optional; prefer instance_iri for detailed steps).
